questions.question: Under United States law, an investigator's notebook may be used in court in which of the following scenarios?

options.A: When the investigator is unwilling to testify.
options.B: To refresh the investigators memory while testifying.
options.D: When otherforms of physical evidence are not available.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: To refresh the investigators memory while testifying.. The other options are incorrect: A (When the investigator is unwilling to testify.); C (Ifthe defense has no objections.); D (When otherforms of physical evidence are not available.).

questions.question: In addition to Human Resources, with what companyfunction must the collection of physical evidence be coordinated ifan employee is suspected?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Legal. The other options are incorrect: A (Industrial Security); C (Public Relations); D (External Audit Group).

questions.question: To be admissible in court, computer evidence must be which of the following?

options.C: They have had previous contact with law enforcement
options.D: They deviate from the accepted norms of society

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: They hold a position oftrust. The other options are incorrect: B (They conspire with others); C (They have had previous contact with law enforcement); D (They deviate from the accepted norms of society).

questions.question: Once evidence is seized, a law enforcement officer should emphasize which of the
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Chain of custody. The other options are incorrect: B (Chain ofcommunications); C (Chain of control); D (Chain of command).

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be undertaken in conjunction orwhile computer incident handling is ongoing?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: System development activity. The other options are incorrect: A (Risk management process); B (System); D (Help-deskfunction).

questions.question: Which of the following addresses industrial and corporate espionage?

options.A: 1996 U.S Economic and Protection of Proprietary Information Act
options.C: 1980 Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD)
options.D: 1970 U.S. Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organization Act (RICO)

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: 1996 U.S Economic and Protection of Proprietary Information Act. The other options are incorrect: B (Generally Accepted Systems Security); C (1980 Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD)); D (1970 U.S. Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organization Act (RICO)).

questions.question: Which of the following provides for data collection limitations, the quality of data, specifications of the purpose for data collection, limitations of data use, participation by the individual on whom the data is being collected, and accountability of the data controller?

options.A: 1973 U.S. Code of Fair Information Practices
options.D: 1980 Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD) Guidelines

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the principles within the Generally Accepted Information Security

options.A: System owners have security responsibilities outside their organizations.
options.B: Computer security responsibilities and accountability should be made explicit.
options.C: Computer security is constrained by societal
options.D: Computer security is ultimately the responsibility of upper management.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Computer security is ultimately the responsibility of upper management.. The other options are incorrect: A (System owners have security responsibilities outside their organizations.); B (Computer security responsibilities and accountability should be made explicit.); C (Computer security is constrained by societal).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT part of the

options.A: Not misuse the information in which they come into contact duringthe course of their duties, and they shall maintain the confidentiality of all information in their possession that is so
options.B: Appropriately report activity related to the profession that they believe to be unlawful and shall cooperate with resulting
options.C: Execute responsibilities in a manner consistent with the highest standards of their
options.D: Not use a computerto harm people or interfere with other people's computer work.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Not use a computerto harm people or interfere with other people's computer work.. The other options are incorrect: A (Not misuse the information in which they come into contact duringthe course of their duties, and they shall maintain the confidentiality of all information in their possession that is so); B (Appropriately report activity related to the profession that they believe to be unlawful and shall cooperate with resulting); C (Execute responsibilities in a manner consistent with the highest standards of their).

questions.question: Which of the following is from the Internet Architecture Board (IAB) Ethics and the Internet (RFC 1087)?

options.A: Access to and use of the Internet is a privilege and should be treated as such by all
options.B: There must not be personal data record-keeping systems whose very existence
options.C: There must be a wayfor a person to prevent information about them, which was obtained for one purpose, from being used or made available for another purpose without their consent.
options.D: Users should execute responsibilities in a manner consistent with the highest standards of their profession.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Access to and use of the Internet is a privilege and should be treated as such by all. The other options are incorrect: B (There must not be personal data record-keeping systems whose very existence); C (There must be a wayfor a person to prevent information about them, which was obtained for one purpose, from being used or made available for another purpose without their consent.); D (Users should execute responsibilities in a manner consistent with the highest standards of their profession.).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT defined in the Internet Architecture Board (IAB) Ethics and the Internet (RFC 1087) as unacceptable and unethical activity?

options.A: involves negligence in the conduct of lnternet-wide experiments
options.C: wastes resources such as people, capacity and computers through such actions

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: uses a computerto steal. The other options are incorrect: A (involves negligence in the conduct of lnternet-wide experiments); B (destroys the integrity of computer-based); C (wastes resources such as people, capacity and computers through such actions).

questions.question: Which one of the following is a key agreement protocol used to enable two entities to agree and generate a session key (secret key used for one session) over an insecure medium without any prior secrets or communications between the entities? The negotiated key
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Diffie_Hellmann. The other options are incorrect: A (3DES).

questions.question: In the process ofgathering evidence from a computer attack, a system administrator took a series of actions which are listed below. Can you identify which one of these actions has compromised the whole evidence collection process?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Displayed the contents of a. The other options are incorrect: A (Using a write blocker); B (Created a message digest for); C (Made a full-disk).

questions.question: Which of the following tools is NOT
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Tripwire. The other options are incorrect: A (Nmap); B (Nessus); D (Saint).

questions.question: Which of the following computer crime is MORE often associated with INSIDERS?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Data diddling. The other options are incorrect: A (IP spoofing); C (Denial of service (DOS)); D (Password sniffing).

questions.question: Which type of attack

options.C: Dictionary attack against an encrypted password

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Dictionary attack against an encrypted password. The other options are incorrect: A (Social engineering); B (Dumpster diving); D (Password sniffing).

questions.question: What do the ILOVEYOU and Melissa virus attacks have in common?

options.B: They are both social engineering attacks.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: They are both masquerading attacks.. The other options are incorrect: B (They are both social engineering attacks.); C (They are both denial-of-service (DOS) attacks.); D (They have nothing in common.).

questions.question: Crackers today are MOSToften interested in

options.D: Helping the community in securing their networks.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Gaining Money or Financial Gains.. The other options are incorrect: B (Getting recognition for their actions.); C (Seeing how fartheir skills); D (Helping the community in securing their networks.).

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding trade secrets is FALSE

options.A: A resource can be protected by law if it is not generally known and if it requires special
options.B: The Trade Secret Law normally protects the expression of the idea of the resource.
options.C: Many companies require their employees to sign nondisclosure agreements regarding the protection of their trade secrets.
options.D: For a company to have a resource qualify as a trade secret, it must provide the company with some type of competitive value or advantage.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: The Trade Secret Law normally protects the expression of the idea of the resource.. The other options are incorrect: A (A resource can be protected by law if it is not generally known and if it requires special); C (Many companies require their employees to sign nondisclosure agreements regarding the protection of their trade secrets.); D (For a company to have a resource qualify as a trade secret, it must provide the company with some type of competitive value or advantage.).

questions.question: What is the PRIMARY goal of incident handling?

options.A: Improve the company's abilityto be prepared for threats and disasters
options.B: lmprove the company's disaster recovery plan
options.C: Contain and repair any damage caused by an event
options.D: Successfully retrieve all evidence that can be used to prosecute

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Contain and repair any damage caused by an event. The other options are incorrect: A (Improve the company's abilityto be prepared for threats and disasters); B (lmprove the company's disaster recovery plan); D (Successfully retrieve all evidence that can be used to prosecute).

questions.question: Which of the following would be LESS likelyto prevent an employee from reporting an incident?

options.A: The process of reporting incidents is centralized.
options.B: They are afraid of being pulled into something they don't want to be involved with.
options.C: They are unaware of the company's security policies and procedures.
options.D: They are afraid of being accused of something they didn't do.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: The process of reporting incidents is centralized.. The other options are incorrect: B (They are afraid of being pulled into something they don't want to be involved with.); C (They are unaware of the company's security policies and procedures.); D (They are afraid of being accused of something they didn't do.).

questions.question: Which of the following outlined how senior management are responsible for the computer and information security decisions that they make and what actually took place within their organizations?

options.C: The Computer Fraud and Abuse Act of 1986.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: The Federal Sentencing Guidelines of 1991.. The other options are incorrect: A (The Economic Espionage Act of 1996.); C (The Computer Fraud and Abuse Act of 1986.); D (The Computer Security Act of 1987.).

questions.question: What is the PRIMARY reason to maintain the chain of custody on evidence that has been collected?

options.A: To ensure that no evidence is
options.B: To ensure that incidents were handled with due care and due diligence.
options.D: To ensure that all possible evidence is gathered.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: To ensure that. The other options are incorrect: A (To ensure that no evidence is); B (To ensure that incidents were handled with due care and due diligence.); D (To ensure that all possible evidence is gathered.).

questions.question: Which of the following logical access exposures INVOLVES CHANGING data before, or as it is entered into the computer?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Data diddling. The other options are incorrect: B (Viruses); C (Trojan horses); D (Salami techniques).

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an active attack?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Scanning. The other options are incorrect: A (Eavesdropping); B (Wiretapping); C (Traffic analysis).

questions.question: The criteria for evaluating the legal requirements for implementing safeguards is to evaluate the cost (C) of instituting the protection versus the estimated loss (L) resulting from the exploitation of the corresponding vulnerability. Therefore, a legal
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A. The other options are incorrect: C (C>L-(residual risk)); D (C< L-(residual risk)).

questions.question: What is called an exception to the search warrant requirement that allows an officer to conduct a search without having the warrant in-hand if probable cause is present and destruction of the evidence is deemed imminent?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Exigent Circumstance Doctrine. The other options are incorrect: A (Evidence Circumstance Doctrine); C (Exigent Probable Doctrine); D (Evidence ofAdmissibility Doctrine).

questions.question: Which of the following proves or disproves a specific act through oral testimony based on information gathered through the witness'sfive senses?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Direct evidence. The other options are incorrect: A (Circumstantial evidence); B (Conclusive evidence); C (Corroborative evidence).

questions.question: Evidence that is not based on personal, firsthand knowledge of the witness, but was obtained from another source is called what?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Hearsay evidence. The other options are incorrect: A (Circumstantial evidence); B (Secondary evidence); C (Conclusive evidence).

questions.question: This is a common security issue that is extremely hard to control in large environments. It occurs when a user has more computer rights, permissions, and access than what is required for the tasks the user needs to fulfill. What best describes this scenario?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Excessive Privileges. The other options are incorrect: A (Excessive Permissions); C (Excessive Access); D (Excessive Rights).

questions.question: Phreakers are hackers who specialize in telephone fraud. What type oftelephone fraud/attack makes use of a device that generates tones to simulate inserting coins in pay phones, thus fooling the system into completingfree calls?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Red Boxes. The other options are incorrect: A (White Boxes); B (Blue Boxes); D (Black Boxes).

questions.question: When companies come togetherto work in an integrated manner such as extranets, special care must be taken to ensure that each party promises to provide the necessary level of protection

This type of supporting evidence is used to help prove an idea or a point, however It cannot stand on its own, it is used as a supplementarytool to help prove a primary piece of evidence. What is the name ofthis type ofevidence?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Corroborative evidence. The other options are incorrect: A (Secondary evidence); B (Circumstantial evidence); D (Opinion evidence FQJETE).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Trade Secrets F-jl$JZ~ZI;. The other options are incorrect: A (Copyright Fdi); B (Trademark); D (Patent).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C. The other options are incorrect: A (Administrative law EBUZ); B (Public); D (Criminal).

questions.question: What category of law deals with regulatory standards that regulate performance and conduct? Government agencies create these standards, which are usually applied to companies and individuals within those companies?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: Administrative. The other options are incorrect: B (Standards); C (Compliance); D (Conduct).

questions.question: The copyright law ("original works of authorship") protects the right of the owner in all of the following except?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: The. The other options are incorrect: A (The public distribution of the); B (Display of the); D (Reproduction of the).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Methods. The other options are incorrect: A (Means); B (Opportunities Hi/i); C (Motivation iii).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Black Boxes. The other options are incorrect: A (Red Boxes); C (White Boxes); D (Blue Boxes).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Exc|usionary rule. The other options are incorrect: A (lnvestigation rule); B (Hearsay rule); D (Best evidence rule).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: Second hand evidenceZilE. The other options are incorrect: A (Best); B (Demonstrative evidenceiEl5UliETE); C (Direct evidencefl).

questions.question: Which of the following would be MOST important to guarantee that the computer evidence

options.A: The chain of custody of the evidence must show who collected, secured, controlled, handled, transported the evidence, and that it was nottampered with.
options.B: It must prove a fact that is immaterial to the case.
options.C: The process for producing it must be documented and repeatable.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A: The chain of custody of the evidence must show who collected, secured, controlled, handled, transported the evidence, and that it was nottampered with.. The other options are incorrect: B (It must prove a fact that is immaterial to the case.); C (The process for producing it must be documented and repeatable.); D (Its reliability must be proven.).

options.C: Internet Activities Board's Ethics and the Internet (RFC1087)

questions.question: Which of the following European Union (EU) principles pertaining to the protection of information on private individuals is incorrect?

options.A: Individuals have the right to correct errors contained in their personal data.
options.B: Data collected by an organization can be used for any purpose and for as long as necessary, as long as it is never communicated outside of the organization by which
options.C: Transmission of personal information to locations where "equivalent" personal data protection cannot be assured is prohibited.
options.D: Records kept on an individual should be accurate and up to date.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is B: Data collected by an organization can be used for any purpose and for as long as necessary, as long as it is never communicated outside of the organization by which. The other options are incorrect: A (Individuals have the right to correct errors contained in their personal data.); C (Transmission of personal information to locations where "equivalent" personal data protection cannot be assured is prohibited.); D (Records kept on an individual should be accurate and up to date.).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Generally Accepted System Security

options.A: Systems owners have security responsibilities outside their organization.
options.B: Computer security supports the mission of the organization
options.C: The conception ofcomputerviruses and worms is unethical.
options.D: C iomputer security should be cost-effective

The other options are incorrect: A (Systems owners have security responsibilities outside their organization.); B (Computer security supports the mission of the organization); D (C iomputer security should be cost-effective).

questions.question: Which of the following would best describe secondary evidence?

options.A: Oral testimony by an expert witness
options.B: Evidence that proves a specific act
options.C: Oral testimony by a non-expert witness
options.D: A copy of a piece of evidence

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D: A copy of a piece of evidence. The other options are incorrect: A (Oral testimony by an expert witness); B (Evidence that proves a specific act); C (Oral testimony by a non-expert witness).
